Site.defaultTitleSuffix: Ten rooms in the highlands of Madagascar
Site.description: A small ten-room hotel in the highlands of Fianarantsoa, Madagascar. On the RN7, 12 km from town. Opened in September 2014, on the family domain established in 2002.

Common.freeCancellation30: Free cancellation up to 10 days before arrival.

Footer.newsletterBody: A letter each season. The garden, the kitchen, the travellers who came through. Sometimes a room before everyone else, sometimes a rate kept for readers.
Footer.newsletterDone: Noted. You'll be among the first to hear.

Hero.subtitle: Open since September 2014, twelve kilometres north of Fianarantsoa.

Stay.intro: The same sand walls, the same dark wood floors, the same hot water bottles at night. What changes: the size, the view, the bed.

Dining.name: Toko Telo.
Dining.intro: Fifty covers. Produce from the Tantsaha, the market gardeners next door. Malagasy, French and Norwegian cooking on one menu.
dishes: Zebu Marengo, garden herbs and Fianar wine

Experiences.h2: To walk, to weave, to cook. What this land gives, when it gives it.

Story.bodyLead: The land was acquired in 2002 — a place called Vonimboahirana, once a family meeting ground. A chalet for the first travellers followed in 2003, the garden kitchen Villa Hagen in 2005. From Norway, the idea of
Story.bodyTrail: — an attention to the small warm things of the everyday. Hôtel Ambalakely opened officially in September 2014. Run as a family: the garden, the kitchen, the welcome.
Story.quote: The hotel is as much our family home as it is a place for guests.
Story.imageAlt: Main building of Hôtel Ambalakely, Fianarantsoa

Trust.h2: A share of every stay supports the school next door.
Trust.body: Hope for the Future opened in 2014. One hundred and thirty children from Tanambao, the Ambalakely neighbourhood. Ten years of the same Wednesday and Saturday afternoons.
Trust.imageAlt: Hope for the Future, the school of the Tanambao neighbourhood

Location.h2: On Route Nationale 7, halfway between the capital and the south.
Location.byRoadBody: Private transfer along the RN7. We arrange it from your arrival at Ivato International.
Location.fromCentreBody: Twelve kilometres north along a quiet road. The hotel sits on the hill above the village.
Location.byAirBody: Domestic flights to Fianarantsoa airport from Antananarivo, then a 25-minute drive.

Journal.kicker: Seasonal notes — the kitchen, the garden, the highlands.
Journal.metaDescription: The journal of Hôtel Ambalakely. Hasina and Mamy on cooking, the garden, the highlands and the people who pass through.
Journal.introLead: Writings by Hasina and Mamy. The kitchen, the garden, the people who pass through.
Journal.emptyH2: The first pieces will arrive this season.
Journal.emptyBody: Hasina is writing. The kitchen, the garden, the highlands. A few paragraphs at a time, when the day allows. Leave us your email and you'll be the first to read.
Journal.moreEverySeason: New writing each season. Sign up for the newsletter at reception when you arrive.

Book.body: Two ways to reach us. The form for dates and rooms. WhatsApp or email if you'd rather write. Hasina or Mamy reply personally.
Book.freeCancellation: Free cancellation up to 10 days before arrival.
Book.whatsappMessage: Hello, I'd like to book a stay at Ambalakely.

BookingDrawer.name: Name
BookingDrawer.messagePlaceholder: Anything we should know — preferences, occasions, questions.
BookingDrawer.footer: Free cancellation up to 10 days before arrival. For groups, we combine rooms — write to us.
BookingDrawer.groupCTA: For groups, we combine rooms and tailor the stay. Write to us directly.
BookingDrawer.successBody: Thank you. We will reply personally with availability and a tailored quote. A confirmation has been sent to your inbox. For travel that's imminent, WhatsApp is the fastest way to reach us.
BookingDrawer.datesHint: Click to select arrival and departure

Cookie.title: Cookies

Cookie.categoryAnalyticsDesc: PostHog. Pages visited, clicks, anonymised journey.
Cookie.categoryErrorsDesc: Sentry. JavaScript errors and technical incidents.

About.metaDescription: Mamy and Hasina acquired the Vonimboahirana land in 2002. A chalet for visitors in 2003, the Villa Hagen kitchen in 2005, Hôtel Ambalakely opened officially in September 2014. Toko Telo, the house table, in 2015. They also run Trans Groupe Hasina, a travel agency for the past twenty years.
About.imageAlt: Hôtel Ambalakely, highlands of Fianarantsoa, Madagascar
About.heroAlt: Mamy and Hasina, founders of Hôtel Ambalakely in Fianarantsoa, Madagascar
About.introLede: A hotel that feels like a home, on a hill twelve kilometres north of Fianarantsoa.
About.introP1Lead: Mamy and Hasina acquired the Vonimboahirana land at Ambalakely in 2002 — once a family meeting ground. A chalet for the first travellers followed in 2003, the Villa Hagen kitchen in 2005. The foundation stone of Hôtel Ambalakely was laid in April 2013, and the hotel opened officially in September 2014. Ten rooms, a kitchen, a garden, a small team. The idea of the house:
About.introP1Trail: , in the country, in the Betsileo tradition.
About.introP2Lead: Before the hotel, they had already been running the travel agency
About.introP2Trail: for twenty years. The agency still operates today, arranging stays across Madagascar. The hotel has become the natural base for TGH travellers in the highlands.
About.foundersImageAlt: The garden and exterior of Hôtel Ambalakely
About.foundersHasinaBody: studied at the University of Stavanger, in Norway, where she spent five years. She speaks Norwegian and has long ties to the country. She runs the house, writes the menu and welcomes guests.
About.foundersMamyBody: is a deep connoisseur of Madagascar, from north to south. His network and knowledge of the land have shaped the TGH agency. He looks after the garden, the transfers and the logistics.
About.foundersTogether: Twenty years together in tourism, they work around responsible and sustainable travel, with respect for culture and environment, and support for local communities.
About.timelineH2: Twenty years of tourism, twelve years of hotel.
About.tghH2: Trans Groupe Hasina, for twenty years.
About.tghP1: Trans Groupe Hasina (TGH) is the travel agency Mamy and Hasina founded in Fianarantsoa, in the Betsileo highlands. It arranges stays across Madagascar: the RN7 south, the western Tsingy, the eastern parks, the northern beaches.
About.tghP2: Hôtel Ambalakely has become the natural base for TGH travellers in the region. The two share a team, a philosophy of responsible tourism, and a long bond with Norway — Hasina being the only French- and Norwegian-speaking guide in the region.
About.pullQuote: The hotel is as much our family home as it is a place for travellers.
About.hopeP1: A school for the children of Tanambao, the neighbourhood around Hôtel Ambalakely. One hundred and thirty children. Started before the hotel, still living thanks to it.
About.hopeP2: A share of every booking supports the project.
About.hopeCTA: Read about Hope for the Future
About.hopeImageAlt: Hope for the Future, the Tanambao school
About.bookH2: Two nights or more. The garden gives more to those who linger.

milestones.landBody: Mamy and Hasina acquire the Vonimboahirana plot at Ambalakely, once a family meeting ground. First chalet for visitors in 2003, the garden kitchen Villa Hagen in 2005.
milestones.tghBody: They found Trans Groupe Hasina, a travel agency in Fianarantsoa. Twenty years later, TGH organises journeys across Madagascar with a particular care for responsible travel.
milestones.openBody: Foundation stone laid in April 2013. Hôtel Ambalakely opens officially in September 2014, 12 km north of Fianarantsoa. Ten rooms, a kitchen, a garden, a small team.
milestones.tokoTeloBody: The original chalet becomes Toko Telo — three stones of the hearth, in Malagasy. The house kitchen extends Villa Hagen: produce from the garden and from the Tantsaha next door, where the highlands, Norway, and France quietly cross.
milestones.todayTitle: The family, the team, the house
milestones.todayBody: The hotel and the agency employ people from the Tanambao neighbourhood. Part of every night supports Hope for the Future, the school next door that opened in 2014.

DiningPage.metaDescription: Toko Telo, the restaurant of Hôtel Ambalakely. Fifty covers, Malagasy country cooking with Scandinavian touches. Breakfast, lunch and dinner on site, pizza on the rooftop terrace.
DiningPage.ogDescription: A small kitchen between Madagascar and Norway.
DiningPage.imageAlt: Toko Telo, the dining room of Hôtel Ambalakely
DiningPage.heroAlt: Toko Telo, the restaurant of Hôtel Ambalakely in Fianarantsoa, Madagascar
DiningPage.introLede: A small kitchen between Madagascar and Norway. Fifty seats in the shade of the mango trees, open every day by reservation.
DiningPage.introP1: The idea of the house: Eny ambanivohitra, in the country. Most of what reaches the table comes from the garden behind the kitchen, the rice paddies below, and the local growers (the Tantsaha) a few kilometres away.
DiningPage.introP2: The menu is chosen as one, two or three courses. Upstairs, the terrace runs as a pizzeria all day, open from 10 am to 9.30 pm. Three signature dishes return often: Zebu Marengo, Kjøttkaker, Krumkake.
DiningPage.loungeAlt: The Toko Telo lounge, with its fireplace and stone walls
DiningPage.ctaNote: 24 hours' notice. Please flag allergies the day before.

RoomsPage.metaDescription: Ten rooms across three categories. Supérieure, Confort, Standard. From 182,000 Ariary per night.
RoomsPage.ogDescription: Ten rooms across three categories. Supérieure, Confort, Standard.
RoomsPage.heroAlt: View of the Supérieure rooms over the rice paddies
RoomsPage.introLede: The same sand walls, the same dark wood floors, the same hot water bottles at night. What changes: the size, the view, the bed.
RoomsPage.introP1: Ten rooms across two buildings. Two face the main façade and overlook the rice paddies. Four sit in the middle category, with a writing desk at the window and a king-size bed. Four more are the original rooms, opened with the hotel in 2014. Choose by what you'd like to see from the window.
RoomsPage.ctaNote: Free cancellation up to 10 days before arrival.

ExperiencesPage.metaDescription: Ten things to do from Hôtel Ambalakely. Lemurs at Ranomafana, walks through the rice paddies, cooking, Sahambavy tea, the Andringitra trek, Ambositra carving, a visit to the school, and more.
ExperiencesPage.ogDescription: Ten things to do, all arranged from the house.
ExperiencesPage.imageAlt: View from Hôtel Ambalakely onto the RN7
ExperiencesPage.heroAlt: View of the rice paddies from Hôtel Ambalakely
ExperiencesPage.introLede: Ten things to do from the hotel. All arranged from the house.
ExperiencesPage.introP1: Most guests do two or three. The rice-paddy walk and the school visit are on foot from the hotel. Ranomafana, Sahambavy, Andringitra, Ambositra and Tsaranoro need a driver. We arrange the driver, the park guide, the picnic basket and the tea, in whichever combination suits you.
ExperiencesPage.ctaH2: Book the room. We'll arrange the days.

PlanTrip.metaDescription: Three RN7 itineraries with Hôtel Ambalakely as your base. Three, five and seven days through Madagascar.
PlanTrip.ogDescription: Three RN7 itineraries with Ambalakely as your base.
PlanTrip.imageAlt: View from Hôtel Ambalakely onto the RN7
PlanTrip.heroAlt: Rice paddies below Hôtel Ambalakely on the RN7
PlanTrip.introLede: Three ways to take to the road. Same hotel, different stop.
PlanTrip.introP1: The RN7 crosses Madagascar from north to south, from the capital Antananarivo to the south coast at Tuléar. Most travellers do at least part of it. Ambalakely sits halfway, twelve kilometres north of Fianarantsoa. A comfortable two-night stop for a short trip, three or four for a longer one.
PlanTrip.introP2: Here are three itineraries that work. Mix them as you like. The drivers, transfers and park guides are arranged by the hotel.
PlanTrip.ctaBody: Drivers, park guides, airport transfers, all from the hotel. Tell us your dates and the itinerary that suits you, and we'll put it together.

Community.metaDescription: Hope for the Future: a school for the children of Tanambao, the Ambalakely neighbourhood in Madagascar. 130 children, nine educational and social programmes, a new Akanimamy building built since 2020.
Community.ogTitle: Hope for the Future · Hôtel Ambalakely
Community.ogDescription: A school for the children of Tanambao, supported by every night at the hotel.
Community.imageAlt: Hope for the Future, the children of Tanambao, Ambalakely
Community.heroAlt: Hope for the Future, the children of Tanambao, Ambalakely
Community.introLede: A school for the children of our neighbourhood. One hundred and thirty active children, nine programmes, and a new home called Akanimamy.
Community.introP1: Hope for the Future is the project Mamy and Hasina started in 2014, in the Tanambao neighbourhood of Ambalakely. The village has roughly 4,000 inhabitants, 12 km north of Fianarantsoa.
Community.introP2: They began with a simple question: how many children in the neighbourhood weren't going to public school, or hardly at all? The answer was around a hundred and fifty. By then, Mamy and Hasina had already built the hotel. They knew what the hotel would be funding alongside it.
Community.introP3: Today 130 children take part in the activities. The hotel passes a share of every booking to the association.

timeline.body1: Mamy and Hasina start Hope for the Future with a handful of children from Tanambao.
timeline.body2: Hôtel Ambalakely opens in October. From the very first night, a share of every booking supports the school.
timeline.body3: A 2,000 m² plot is acquired thanks to donations from friends of the project. The first step towards a real home for the children.
timeline.body4: 1 September 2020: the first stone of Akanimamy is laid, a 152 m² building that will become the HFF children's home.
timeline.body5: Nine programmes, loyal volunteers and donors. The walls and roof of Akanimamy are done; sanitation, the kitchen and the sports areas remain.

programs.educationBody: After-school support alongside the public school. Extra classes, learning materials, a hot meal at midday on school days.
programs.healthBody: Basics of self-care and hygiene. Regular weighing and follow-up of the children.
programs.artsBody: Drawing, painting, hands-on work, theatre. The children learn to make.
programs.musicBody: Introduction to flute, violin, piano and guitar.
programs.danceBody: Movement, teamwork. A basketball and volleyball court planned at Akanimamy.
programs.langBody: English clubs, Norwegian lessons, French in the works.
programs.envBody: Ecology awareness, tree planting, a vegetable garden at Akanimamy.
programs.historyBody: General history, Betsileo culture, general knowledge.

Community.akanimamyP1Trail: means sweet. Akanimamy is a cosy nest — the home the children were missing for their homework in peace.
Community.akanimamyP2: The 2,000 m² plot was acquired in 2019, thanks to gifts from friends of the project. The first stone was laid on 1 September 2020. The building covers 152 m², with a main room for activities, a library corner (with books from Norwegian guests and a French collège), a board-games corner, an office and a storeroom.
Community.akanimamyP3: Walls and roof are done. To come: sanitation, the kitchen, basketball and volleyball courts. To plant around: fruit trees, a vegetable garden, Ravintsara.
Community.pullQuote: We knew what the hotel would be funding alongside it before we even opened.
Community.visitP1: Travellers who'd like to can spend a moment at the project during their stay. No formal tour: you meet the team, see the class, have a cup of tea. If you'd like to bring something, please give it directly to the association, not the children.
Community.visitP2: Just mention it when you book. There's no cost.
